tips.title: Master the 50/30/20 Rule
tips.description: Allocate 50% of your income to needs (rent, bills), 30% to wants (hobbies, dining out), and 20% to savings or debt repayment. This simple ratio keeps your finances balanced and sustainable.

tips.title: Track Every Cent with ExpenseMate
tips.description: Recording even the smallest daily expenses helps identify patterns where money 'leaks'. Use our categories to see if that daily coffee is adding up to more than you thought!

tips.title: Build a 3-Month Emergency Fund
tips.description: Financial security starts with a safety net. Aim to save enough to cover 3-6 months of essential living expenses. Keep this in a separate, accessible savings account.

tips.title: The 24-Hour Rule for Impulse Buys
tips.description: Before purchasing anything non-essential over Rs. 2000, wait 24 hours. Often, the initial urge to buy passes, saving you from spending on things you don't truly need.

tips.title: Automate Your Savings
tips.description: Treat your savings like a bill that must be paid. Set up an automatic transfer to your savings account on your payday. If you don't see it in your checking account, you won't spend it.

tips.title: Prioritize High-Interest Debt
tips.description: If you have multiple debts, focus on paying off the ones with the highest interest rates first (usually credit cards). This reduces the total amount you pay over time.

tips.title: Shop with a Grocery List
tips.description: Never go grocery shopping hungry or without a list. Planning meals ahead and sticking to your list prevents impulse purchases and reduces food waste.

tips.title: Review Your Subscriptions
tips.description: Monthly subscriptions for streaming, apps, or gyms can add up quietly. Review them once a month and cancel anything you haven't used in the last 30 days.
